**Tássio Virgínio**[**Federal Institute**|***[tassio.virginio@ifto.edu.br](mailto:tassio.virginio@ifto.edu.br)***of Tocantins] **Luana Martins**[**Federal University**|***[martins.luana@ufba.br](mailto:martins.luana@ufba.br)***of Bahia] **Railana**Santana[**Federal University**|***[railana.santana@ufba.br](mailto:railana.santana@ufba.br)***of Bahia] **Adriana**Cruz**Federal University**|***[adriana.cruz@estudante.ufla.br](mailto:adriana.cruz@estudante.ufla.br)***of Lavras] **Larissa** [**Federal University of Bahia**|***[larissa@ecomp.uefs.br](mailto:larissa@ecomp.uefs.br)***/ State]**Univ. of Feira de Santana** **Heitor**Costa[**Federal University**|***[heitor@ufla.br](mailto:heitor@ufla.br)***of Lavras **Ivan Machado**[**Federal University**|***[ivan.machado@ufba.br](mailto:ivan.machado@ufba.br)***of Bahia]

**Abstract** Several strategies have supported test quality measurement and analysis. For example, code coverage, a widely used one, enables verification of the test case to cover as many source code branches as possible. Another set of affordable strategies to evaluate the test code quality exists, such as test smells analysis. Test choices in test code implementation, and their occurrence might reduce the test suite quality. A practical and large- scale test smells identification depends on automated tool support. Otherwise, testanalysis could become a cost-ineffective strategy.JNoseIn,Testautomatedan earliertoolstudy,supportwe proposedto detectthetest smells and analyze test suite quality from the testperspective. This study extends the previous one in two directions: i)JNose-Corewe implemented, an API encompassingthethe test smells detection rules. Through an extensible architecture, the tool is now capable of accomodating new detection rules or programming languages; and ii) we performed an empiricalJNose Teststudy to evaluateand comparetheit against the state-of- the-arttsDetecttool,. ResultstheshowedJNose-Coreprecisionthat thescore ranges from 91% to 100%, and the recall score from 89% to 100%. It also presented a slight improvement in the test smells detection rules compared to thefor the test smells detection at the class level.

***Keywords:****Tests**Quality, Test Evolution, Test Smells, Evidence-based Software Engineering*

### 1 Introduction

### 7 Related

### Work

build theOracle,theywereexpertswithmorethanthree In large-sized test suites, software engineers barely performyears of experience with test smells. They were aware of the manual detection of test smells. This practice is rather time-test code of the test smellstools. consuming and infeasible in many scenarios. Therefore, the research community has proposed automated tool support for detecting test smells.
